fingerprints_found.description: Kill-shot framework calibration targets are globe-derived great-circle distances
fingerprints_found.detail: The dome's own distance_analysis.py (in scripts/) defines actual_distances as "great circle" distances — explicitly computed on a sphere. The kill-shot Test 1 compares the dome's V13 Finsler prediction against an Indian Pacific railway distance surveyed using globe-based geodetic methods. The dome cannot falsify the globe using measurements defined on a globe. Our Part 5 text notes the calibration problem but does not explicitly flag that the reference distances themselves are globe framework values.
fingerprints_found.globe_value: Great-circle distances (e.g. Sydney-Cape Town: 11,000 km, Sydney-Buenos Aires: 11,700 km)
fingerprints_found.dome_usage: Used as actual_distances comparison targets in the dome's own analysis script
fingerprints_found.already_noted_in_review: partially — our text says the distance was "surveyed" but does not emphasize that the survey used globe geodesy

fingerprints_found.description: V12 Finsler formula hardcodes a=20015 = π×R_Earth to 0.0004% precision
fingerprints_found.detail: distance_analysis.py's dome_distance_ellipse() function uses a=20015 as the semi-major axis. This is π × 6371.0 km = 20015.09 km (the half-circumference of Earth). The dome's fundamental scale parameter IS the globe's half-circumference. Every distance on the dome disc is computed relative to a scale set by globe geometry. This is the single most important structural fingerprint in the entire model — the disc boundary at r=20015 is where an azimuthal equidistant projection from the North Pole maps the South Pole.
fingerprints_found.dome_source_file: scripts/distance_analysis.py line: dome_distance_ellipse(r1, t1, r2, t2, a=20015, b=15000, orient=60)
fingerprints_found.already_noted_in_review: yes — mentioned in multiple WINs. But not explicitly flagged in Part 5 kill-shot section.

fingerprints_found.description: The 0.9941 angular fudge factor has no dome derivation
fingerprints_found.detail: distance_analysis.py applies delta_t = np.radians(t2 - t1) * 0.9941 before computing distances. This 0.9941 factor reduces angular separations by ~0.6% — a correction with no stated derivation from dome physics. It is an empirical fudge that reduces east-west distance overestimation inherent in the azimuthal equidistant projection at lower latitudes. A pure disc geometry would not need this correction; it exists because the underlying coordinate system is a projection of a sphere.
fingerprints_found.globe_value: No correction needed on a sphere (great-circle formula is exact)
fingerprints_found.already_noted_in_review: no — this is a new finding

fingerprints_found.description: compute_r maps Polaris elevation to dome radius — importing spherical alt=lat relationship
fingerprints_found.detail: distance_analysis.py's compute_r(polaris_elev_deg) converts Polaris altitude to dome radial position via the firmament function H(r) = 8537 × exp(-r/8619). The Polaris altitude-latitude relationship is a direct consequence of spherical geometry (on a sphere, Polaris altitude equals observer latitude). The dome imports this relationship wholesale and mediates it through H(r), but the fundamental input (Polaris elevation ≈ geographic latitude) is a globe observable.
fingerprints_found.already_noted_in_review: partially — our Test 2 (Polaris) analysis discusses measurement uncertainty but not the globe origin of the alt=lat input

curmudgeon_c2_review: SEC-5.1.c2.json exists, 8 holes found (all moderate/minor), no c3 review yet

suggested_text_additions.text: All six kill-shot tests are evaluated against measurements made within the globe framework: great-circle distances, globe-calibrated GPS coordinates, INTERMAGNET stations with globe-referenced positions, and NOAA magnetic pole data expressed in geographic latitude/longitude. The dome's distance formula itself uses a=20,015 km — which equals π×R_Earth to 0.0004% — as its fundamental scale parameter. The kill-shot framework tests the dome against data defined on the globe it claims to falsify.
